changes_summary: Revised plan to address all four flags: (1) Redesigned canonical helper to accept a resolved component rather than type+builtInMap, so the preview path (which has a pre-compiled component) can use it without lookup; (2) Changed effectFrames contract to always pass durationInFrames for continuous effects (matching majority behavior in EffectLayerSequence and preview), fixing wrapWithClipEffects to match rather than forcing undefined; (3) Fixed the race condition at the registry level with a post-await code comparison check, with the stale boolean as belt-and-suspenders; (4) Added Step 4 to fix the EffectLayerSequence timing bug using nested Sequences for frame offset compensation.
questions: The EffectLayerSequence timing fix (Step 4) uses nested <Sequence from={-startFrame}> to restore global frame context for children. This is a standard Remotion pattern but changes the component tree structure. Should we add a specific integration test for this, or is the visual smoke test sufficient?

success_criteria.criterion: A single exported wrapWithEffect function exists in effects/index.tsx that accepts a resolved FC<EffectComponentProps> and wraps it with EffectErrorBoundary + validateAndCoerceParams
All three code paths (wrapWithClipEffects, EffectLayerSequence, makePreviewComposition/wrapPreviewEffect) use wrapWithEffect for their effect wrapping
effectFrames contract is consistent everywhere: entrance/exit get computed effectFrames from duration, continuous gets effectFrames=durationInFrames
PREVIEW_FPS constant is removed; inline 30 used as fallback
EffectLayerSequence uses <Sequence from={startFrame}> so continuous effects see local frame 0 at their start, with children wrapped in <Sequence from={-startFrame}> to preserve global frame context
DynamicEffectRegistry.registerAsync checks for superseded registrations after await before writing to prevent stale-compile race
useEffectRegistry useEffect has a stale guard in cleanup to short-circuit remaining loop iterations
All existing tests in src/tools/video-editor/effects/ pass
No regressions in full video-editor test suite (npx vitest run src/tools/video-editor/)
console.log('[EffectLookup]...') per-frame debug logging removed from lookupEffect
EffectLayerSequence.tsx no longer directly imports EffectErrorBoundary or validateAndCoerceParams
EffectCreatorPanel.tsx no longer directly imports EffectErrorBoundary
Each file touched stays under ~300 lines
wrapWithEffect has unit tests covering happy path and error-boundary fallback
registerAsync race condition has a unit test demonstrating the fix
Visual smoke test: preview in EffectCreatorPanel renders correctly for entrance, exit, and continuous effects
Visual smoke test: effect layers starting after frame 0 now animate from their beginning instead of mid-animation
Visual smoke test: timeline render with entrance + continuous + exit effects on a clip renders identically to before

assumptions: The effect application order (continuous innermost → entrance → exit outermost) in wrapWithClipEffects is intentional and correct — will preserve it.
EffectLayerSequence remains its own component (it has unique time-range gating and frame offset logic) — only the inner wrapping is delegated to the canonical helper.
The replaceEffectRegistry call during render in useEffectRegistry is an intentional pattern to keep the global singleton in sync — not a bug to fix.
getSchema is O(1) and does not need per-frame caching — the audit concern about 'registry.getSchema called multiple times per frame' is a non-issue.
Remotion <Sequence from={negative}> works correctly to restore global frame context for children nested inside a frame-offset Sequence. This is a documented Remotion pattern.

The CustomEffectEditor TEMPLATE's effectFrames = 20 default is fine — with the unified contract always passing effectFrames for all effect types, this default is only a fallback for direct component instantiation without the wrapping helper.
